revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Hebizuka Kofun]][[ja:蛇塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Hebizuka Kofun | image = File:Hebizuka Kofun-2.JPG | location = {{ill|Ukyō Ward|qid=Q580186|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.011972|N|135.700167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-26 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | map_size = Mound length 75{{ill|Metre|en|Metre|qid=Q11573|lt=m|1=Metre}} | built = Late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} to early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(Possibly with a {{ill|House-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}) | Interred_individual = (Estimated) Members of the {{ill|Hata clan|en|Hata clan|qid=Q987290|lt=Hata Clan|1=Hata clan}} | burial goods = | Historic site = National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} "Hebizuka Kofun" | notes = The mound itself no longer exists | map = Japan Kyoto city | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest | map_dot_label = bottom | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Hebizuka Kofun''' (蛇塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Uzumasa]]. Its type is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, and it has been Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}}. The site originally contained the largest {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in Kyoto Prefecture among keyhole-shaped Kofuns. However, the mound itself has been lost, leaving only the exposed stone chamber visible today. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Kofuns in the Uzumasa and Sagano Areas{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}} ! Period !! Mound name !! type !! map_size !! Total Chamber Length !! built !! Tomb/Historic site |- | ||{{ill|Tarumi-yama Kofun|ja|垂箕山古墳|1=Tarumi-yama Kofun|2=ja|3=垂箕山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi-yama Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}||65m|| ||Late 5th to early 6th century||Imperial Household Agency-Historic site tomb |- | rowspan=2|3|| rowspan=2|{{ill|Amatsuka Kofun|en|Amatsuka Kofun|qid=Q11442478|lt=Amatsuka Kofun|1=Amatsuka Kofun}}|| rowspan=2|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|| rowspan=2|73m||(8.1m)|| rowspan=2|Early 6th century|| rowspan=2|National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} |- |(7.7m) |- | rowspan=4|4
5||{{ill|Amatsuka_Kofun#Shimizuyama_Kofun|lt=Shimizuyama Kofun|ja|清水山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuka_Kofun#Shimizuyama_Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天塚古墳}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||57m|| || rowspan=2|Mid to late 6th century||(Destroyed) |- |Dan-no-yama Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun|| || ||(Destroyed) |- |{{ill|Narabigaoka 1 Kofun|lt=Narabigaoka 1 Kofun|lt=Futagao-ka No. 1 Kofun|ja|双ヶ岡1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futagao-ka No. 1 Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||44m||15.8m|| rowspan=2|Late 6th century||None |- |Kinugasayama No. 1 Kofun||Round Kofun||26m||(6.0m)||None |- | rowspan=6|6||'''Hebizuka Kofun'''||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||75m||17.8m|| rowspan=6|Late 6th to early 7th century||National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} |- |{{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kyoto)|lt=Maruyama Kofun (Kyoto)|lt=Maruyama Kofun (Kyoto City)|ja|円山古墳 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kyoto City)|qq=}}||Round Kofun||50m||14.7m||Imperial mausoleum reference area |- |{{ill|Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto City)|ja|狐塚古墳 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto City)|qq=}}||Round Kofun||28m||(12.8m)||None |- |{{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|入道塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyudozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大覚寺古墳群}}||{{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||30m|||11.2m||Imperial mausoleum reference area |- |{{ill|Daikakuji Kofun Group|ja|大覚寺古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikakuji Kofuns|qq=}}||Round Kofun||25-30m||8.1m||(Buried) |- |Mido-ga-Ike No. 1 Kofun||Round Kofun||30m||(8.3m)||Kyoto City-registered historic site |- | rowspan=2|7||{{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)|lt=Kozuka Kofun (Kyoto City)|ja|甲塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kozuka Kofun (Kyoto City)|qq=}}||Round Kofun||38m||14.4m|| rowspan=2|Early 7th century||None |- |Hirosawa Kofun||Round Kofun||30m||12.0m||None |}
Located in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|1=Kyoto Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10883263}} (Yamashiro Basin) on the southern edge of the Sagano Plateau, the name "Hebizuka" (Snake Mound) is said to derive from the presence of snakes inhabiting the stone chamber{{R|Explanatory signboard}}. Around 1920 (Taisho 9), part of the mound still remained, but most of it was lost to residential development, leaving the stone chamber exposed{{R|National Designation}}. To date, no excavations have been conducted. The Kofun is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular section facing southwest. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, which opens southeastward. Constructed using massive stones, the total chamber length is 17.8 meters, comparable to the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|en|Ishibutai Kofun|qid=Q3816159|lt=Ishibutai Kofun|1=Ishibutai Kofun}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, [[Asuka Village]]. The chamber is said to have housed a {{ill|House-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}{{Sfn|Hebizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The exact burial goods are unknown. The construction period is estimated to be the late [[Kofun period]] to the {{ill|Terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terminal Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} (late 6th century to early 7th century){{Sfn|Hebizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}. This is one of the latest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture. In the Sagano area, late 6th-century Kofuns such as {{ill|Amatsuka Kofun|en|Amatsuka Kofun|qid=Q11442478|lt=Amatsuka Kofun|1=Amatsuka Kofun}} (approx. 71 meters, national historic site), {{ill|Amatsuka_Kofun#Shimizuyama_Kofun|lt=Shimizuyama Kofun|ja|清水山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuka_Kofun#Shimizuyama_Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天塚古墳}} (approx. 60 meters, destroyed), {{ill|Tarumi-yama Kofun|ja|垂箕山古墳|1=Tarumi-yama Kofun|2=ja|3=垂箕山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi-yama Kofun|qq=}} (Nakano Imperial Prince’s Tomb Kofun, approx. 75 meters), and Hebizuka Kofun are distributed{{R|National Designation}}. These mounds are believed to be related to the activities of the immigrant Hata clan, who developed the Sagano area, and the main mounds are thought to be the clan's chief tombs. The stone chamber area was Historic site a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} in 1977 (Showa 52). Today, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. == Kofun Specifications == The estimated map_size of the Kofun is as follows{{Sfn|Hebizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}: * Mound length: Approx. 75 meters * Rear circular section diameter: Approx. 45 meters * Front rectangular section width: Approx. 30 meters Although the mound has been lost, traces of its original type remain in the surrounding residential plots{{R|National Designation}}. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Hebizuka Kofun - Designated on May 4, 1977 (Showa 52){{National Cultural Property Database|401|1736|Hebizuka Kofun}}. == Access == * A 7-minute walk south from [[Katabira-no-Tsuji Station]] on the {{ill|Keifuku Electric Railroad|en|Keifuku Electric Railroad|qid=Q3537126|lt=Keifuku Electric Railroad|1=Keifuku Electric Railroad}} (Arashiyama Line and Kitano Line). == References == {{References help}} {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Explanatory signboard (Installed by Kyoto City) * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=Hebizuka Kofun|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/91035 Kyoto City Excavation Monitoring Report]|number=Showa 63|publisher=Kyoto City Department of Cultural Affairs|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kyoto City Excavation Monitoring Report|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=Harada Michio|year=1989|chapter=Hebizuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Publishing]]|isbn=978-4-490-10260-4|ref={{Harvid|Hebizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2004|chapter=Historic site - Hebizuka Kofun|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kyotofu-maibun.or.jp/data/kankou/kankou-pdf/jyouhou/kyoutofu-J94.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Archaeological Intypeation]}}|number=94|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Archaeological Research Center|isbn=|pages=37-38|ref={{Harvid|Kyoto Prefecture Archaeological Intypeation Vol. 94|2004}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Hebizuka Kofun}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|1736|Hebizuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:蛇塚古墳]] [[ja:藻塚古坙]] {{Commons category|Hebizuka Kofun}} [[en:Hebizuka Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q11625508}} [[Category:Hata clan]] [[Category:History of Ukyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Regions of Uzumasa]] [[Category:Uzumasa-Sagano Kofun Group]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
